message:
feat(http/h1): chunked transfer-encoding support + security hardening

Client codec now properly decodes HTTP/1.1 chunked responses:
- Added ClientDecodeState::Chunked variant for stateful chunked decoding
- Implemented is_chunked() header detection with comma-separated parsing
- decode_chunked() accumulates chunks until terminal 0\r\n\r\n marker
- New test: decode_chunked_response validates "hello world" reconstruction

Server codec security hardening (RFC 7230 compliance):
- AmbiguousBodyLength error rejects requests with both Content-Length
  and Transfer-Encoding headers (request smuggling prevention per 3.3.3)
- decode_chunked() now enforces max_body_size during chunk accumulation
- Added CRLF validation after chunk data and terminal chunk
- New max_body_size() builder method for configurable limits

Comprehensive test coverage:
- decode_body_too_large_content_length: rejects oversized Content-Length
- decode_body_too_large_chunked: rejects chunked bodies exceeding limit
- decode_body_at_limit_succeeds: boundary condition validation
- reject_both_content_length_and_transfer_encoding: smuggling protection
- reject_invalid_crlf_after_chunk: malformed chunked encoding detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
